name: mongodb-js__mongodb-mcp-server
description: Official MongoDB MCP server that enables AI assistants to interact with MongoDB databases and Atlas deployments. Supports CRUD operations, aggregation pipelines, collection management, indexing, and Atlas cluster administration.
instructions:
Archestra supports two authentication methods:

**1. Connection String**

Set `MDB_MCP_CONNECTION_STRING` with your MongoDB connection string.

**2. X.509 Auth**

Requires a connection string plus `tls_certificate_key` and `tls_certificate_ca` secrets. See [MongoDB X.509 docs](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/tutorial/configure-x509-client-authentication/).

tools.name: listCollections
tools.description: List all collections in a database

tools.name: createIndex
tools.description: Create an index on a collection

tools.name: dropIndex
tools.description: Drop an index from a collection

tls_certificate_key.title: TLS certificate key for the X.509 auth
tls_certificate_key.description: Paste your TLS certificate key here. Creates secret at the /secrets/tls_certificate_key path, which is referenced by MDB_MCP_TLS_CERTIFICATE_KEY_FILE

tls_certificate_ca.title: CA certificate for the X.509 auth.
tls_certificate_ca.description: Paste your CA certificate here. Creates secret at the /secrets/tls_certificate_ca path, which is referenced by MDB_MCP_TLS_CERTIFICATE_CA_FILE

readme:
# MongoDB MCP Server

The official MongoDB MCP server provides tools for interacting with MongoDB databases and Atlas deployments through AI assistants.

## Features

- **CRUD Operations**: Find, insert, update, and delete documents
- **Aggregation**: Execute aggregation pipelines for data analysis
- **Collection Management**: Create, drop, and rename collections
- **Index Management**: Create, drop, and list indexes
- **Schema Inference**: Analyze collection schemas
- **Atlas Integration**: Manage Atlas clusters, users, and access lists

## Environment Variables

- `MDB_MCP_CONNECTION_STRING`: MongoDB connection string for database access
- `MDB_MCP_READ_ONLY`: Enable read-only mode (optional)
- `MDB_MCP_API_CLIENT_ID`: Atlas API client ID (optional)
- `MDB_MCP_API_CLIENT_SECRET`: Atlas API client secret (optional)
